title: Rich Jamaican Beef Bake
description: A gooey, wood-fired beef bake bursting with Jamaican spices, served in a Talavera plate for an adventurous, gluten-free dinner.
instructions: In a large skillet, heat 2 tbsp oil over medium-high heat and sear the beef cubes until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Remove the beef and set aside.
In the same skillet, sauté the diced onion, minced garlic, and sliced carrots until softened, about 4 minutes.
Return the beef to the skillet, add diced tomatoes, allspice, thyme, brown sugar, salt, and pepper; stir to combine.
Pour in the beef broth, bring to a simmer, cover, and let cook for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Transfer the mixture to a Talavera plate, cover with foil, and bake in the preheated oven for 45 minutes until the beef is tender and the sauce has thickened.
Remove the foil, stir the bake, and bake for an additional 10 minutes to develop a gooey, caramelized surface.
Let the dish rest for 5 minutes before serving; pair with steamed rice or a fresh salad.
